portfolio_rationale: GOOGL leads with the cleanest bull case: zero bearish technical factors, strong fundamentals, low debt, and a bear case that relied on contradicted data. LLY earns the second slot with consistent 3-week bullish signals, exceptional 36% revenue growth, and broadly positive news sentiment despite leverage risk. AAPL and AMZN receive smaller allocations as solid but less differentiated bullish cases with recurring risk flags; XOM gets a minimal allocation given its thin fundamental case and six risk flags; BRK.B is excluded due to bearish technicals, negative revenue growth, and the weakest judge confidence in the group.

GOOGL.reason: Cleanest bull case with zero bearish technical factors, strong ROE/ROA, low leverage, and a bear rebuttal that was clearly outargued.

LLY.reason: Three consecutive bullish judge signals, 36% revenue growth, majority-positive news flow, and strong margins outweigh the elevated leverage concern.

AAPL.reason: Strong profitability and clean technical sweep support the bull case, though zero revenue growth and recurring liquidity flags limit conviction relative to top picks.

AMZN.reason: 10% revenue growth and improving momentum are compelling, but recurring weakening-momentum risk flags and three prior neutral signals reduce relative conviction.

XOM.reason: Positive technicals and solid FCF support a modest position, but six risk flags including negative 20-day return momentum and thin fundamental disclosure cap the allocation.

BRK.B.reason: Bearish technical analyst signal, negative revenue growth, price below key moving averages, and the lowest judge confidence in the universe make this the weakest bull case.
